entries.name: 5-amino-1MQ
entries.benefits: • Assists with weight loss • Helps with muscle preservation • Body recomposition agent • Lowers cholesterol levels • Fights inflammation • Enhances aged muscle regeneration
entries.protocols: Treatment length: 20-30 days followed by 1-2 weeks off Oral: • 50-150 mg daily with food (Jay Campbell) • 150 mg daily, 30 minutes after taking NAD+ (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog)
entries.sideEffects: • Difficulty sleeping • Difficulty participating in exercise

entries.name: AOD-9604
entries.benefits: • Used for weight loss • Is believed to help reduce body fat and to help preventing new fat growth thus improving the body composition • Fat burner • Stimulates cartilage growth and repair, which makes it a potential treatment for osteoarthritis
entries.protocols: Treatment length:  Most popular is cycling 20 days on, 10 days off for up to 6 months SQ:  • 300 mcg in the morning while fasted (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog, Jay Campbell) • 250 mcg twice a day at morning (while fasted) and bedtime (1-4 hours after meal) (Dr. Seeds) Oral:  • Dissolve 500 mcg troche under tongue in the morning
entries.notes: AOD stands for Anti Obesity Drug • Modified form of Frag 176-191 • May need a month for the effects to kick in • Use for minimum of 3 months • Some think it’s not cost-effective given how long it takes to see the results • Recently all major vendors started having issue with AOD turning into jelly. Some solce it by adding more BAC water, some throw away the via
entries.sideEffects: • Diarrhea • Flatulence • Increased appetite • Mild or moderate headache • Nausea

entries.name: ARA 290
entries.benefits: • Promotes tissue repair • Relieves neuropathic pain • Fights diabetes  • Boosts immune function
entries.protocols: Treatment length: 4-5 weeks SQ: • 1-5 mg daily (Muscle + Brawn)
entries.notes: • Soluble in alkaline solutions. May appear cloudy when it’s mixed with BAC water
entries.sideEffects: • Elevated blood pressure • Fast heart rate • Liver enzyme elevation • Nausea/vomiting

entries.name: Argireline
entries.benefits: • Skin anti-age benefits • Extends the effect of anti-wrinkle injections
entries.sideEffects: • Dry, flaky skin • Redness • Stinging sensation

entries.name: BPC-157
entries.benefits: • Wound healing, soft tissue injury recovery, reduce inflammation • Bone, joint, tendons and ligaments health • GI health (helps with gastric ulcers) • Normalizing blood pressure • Strengthens the immune system • Positively impacts mood, behavior, and cognitive health • Exhibits potential anti-cancer properties • Stimulates formation of new blood vessels • Helps with the scar tissue formation
entries.protocols: Treatment length: Not for long term 30 days (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog) 8 weeks, followed by a 2-week rest. Can repeat another cycle if needed (World of Peptides) SQ:  • Start slow with 100 mcg daily • 300 mcg daily (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog) • 250 mcg twice a day (reports from Discord and Reddit) • 200-300 mcg twice a day at the location of pain/injury (Metabolic Optimization Guide) Oral:  • 500 mcg capsules twice a day IM:  • 150 mcg twice a day at the location of pain/injury (World of Peptides)
entries.notes: • BPC stand for Body Protective Compound
entries.sideEffects: • Changes in blood pressure • Changes in heart rhythm • Dizziness • Fatigue • Hot flashes • Nausea and vomiting • Can affect mood if taking with SSRI • Discontinue if causes depression or anxiety symptoms

sources.note: ANIMAL DATA + 2-subject human pilot. Mateescu et al., Pharmaceutics 2026;18(5):625 (peer-reviewed review): formal preclinical ADME in two species confirms a 'sub-30-min plasma half-life, linear dose-proportional kinetics, and intramuscular bioavailability of 14-51% depending on species'; a 'plasma half-life of under 30 min' was 'confirmed preclinically and in a preliminary two-subject human pilot'. 0.5 h is the published UPPER BOUND, not a measured mean. The dataset's current 4 h value is not supported by any source found.

Follows directly from the sub-30-min plasma half-life. Note the same review flags a PK/PD disconnect: plasma clearance is fast but biological effects persist hours to days, so a plasma-concentration curve will understate duration of effect.

entries.name: CJC-1295
entries.benefits: • Increases muscle mass and strength • Improves bone density • Increases lean mass • Increases overall energy and well-being • Used for burns or significant soft-tissue injury, post surgery • Improves sleep quality • Improves cognitive function
entries.protocols: Treatment length: 30 days Wait 2-3 hours after eating, and don’t eat for another 30 minutes after injection  CJC-1295 DAC SQ: • 100 mcg twice a week (Dr. Seeds)  CJC-1295 no DAC SQ: • 200 mcg nightly (5 nights on, 2 nights off) (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog)  CJC-1295 no DAC is often stacked with Ipamorelin: SQ: • 200mcg/200mcg nightly (5 nights on, 2 nights off) (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog) Dr. Seeds claims that 100mcg/100mcg is more cost-effective and higher dose is only marginally better.
entries.halfLife: • CJC-1295 no DAC: 30 minutes • CJC-1295 DAC: 6-8 days
entries.notes: • Also known as MOD GRF (1-29) • Human growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH). GHRH should be paired with CHRP, as it increases the GHRP effect and you can use a lower dose • CJC-1295 no DAC has shorter half-life, so more frequent injections are needed • CJC-1295 DAC has longer half-life, so fewer injections are needed
entries.sideEffects: • Water retention • Tingling • Numbness and decreased insulin  • Tiredness, feeling of lethargy • Head rush or euphoria • Itching

sources.note: HUMAN. Ionescu M, Frohman LA. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2006;91(12):4792-7: CJC-1295 'binds permanently to endogenous albumin after injection (half-life = 8 d)'. CONFLICTING/COMPLEMENTARY: Teichman SL et al., J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2006;91(3):799-805 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16352683/) estimated the half-life at 5.8-8.1 days in healthy adults after subcutaneous dosing. 192 h = 8 d is the explicitly published single value; the maintainer may prefer ~168 h (7 d) as the centre of Teichman's range. THIS IS THE DAC FORM ONLY.

HUMAN. Teichman 2006: after a single subcutaneous injection there were 'dose-dependent increases in mean plasma GH concentrations by 2- to 10-fold for 6 d or more and in mean plasma IGF-I concentrations by 1.5- to 3-fold for 9-11 d'. 6 days is the conservative (GH) figure; the IGF-1 effect ran 9-11 days.
Albumin-bound DAC form, half-life ~6-8 days, dosed weekly/biweekly in the Teichman trials.

entries.name: Dihexa
entries.benefits: • Combats Parkinson’s and Alzheimer’s disease • Improves learning and memory retention  • Improves stroke recovery  • Accelerates recovery from spinal cord injury and nerve damage • Protects against hearing loss
entries.protocols: Topical: • 20 mg/ml twice a day (Dr. Seeds) • 8-45 mg daily (Jay Campbell) • 10-20 mg to inner forearms daily, rub in until absorbed (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog)  Oral: • 1-2 mg daily (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog)
entries.sideEffects: • Anxiety • Change of taste • Insomnia • Irritability • Mood swings • Nausea

entries.name: DSIP
entries.benefits: • Improves Sleep Quality  • Reduces Chronic Pain  • Fights Stress  • Boosts Brain Power  • Treats Depression  • Lowers the Risk of Heart Disease • Lowers Blood Pressure  • Prevents Cancer and Reduces the Side Effects of Chemotherapy • Treats Symptoms of Diabetes • Provides Support for Alcohol and Drug Withdrawal
entries.protocols: SQ: • 100 mcg three hours before bedtime (Dr. Seeds, Peptide Therapy) • 250 mcg (some may need up to 450 mcg) at least two hours before bedtime (World of Peptides) • Can be given daily, every 3 days, weekly
entries.notes: • Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide
entries.sideEffects: • Headache • Hypotension (low blood pressure) • Vagal reactions (dizziness, ringing ears, and sweating) • Vomiting

sources.note: ANIMAL DATA. Kato N et al., Neuroendocrinology 1984;39(1):39-44: DSIP showed 'a rapid disappearance with a mean metabolic clearance rate of 30.7 +/- 2.5 ml/kg.min and a mean half-life of 4.0 +/- 0.7 min in the dogs', with 2.9 min in a monkey and 2.0 +/- 0.54 min in rats. 0.067 h = 4 min (dog). NO HUMAN PK EXISTS. This conflicts with the dataset's current 7-8 minute claim, for which no source could be located.
Corroborated qualitatively in Int J Mol Sci 2026;27:3890 peer-reviewed review: 'DSIP has a half-life of minutes and is rapidly eliminated'.

entries.name: Epitalon (Epithalon, Epithalamin)
entries.benefits: • Slows down aging • Suppresses tumor development • Stimulates melatonin production, helping to regulate sleep cycles • Prevents cardiovascular disease • Boosts brain health • Improves eye health • Boosts immunity • Treats symptoms of diabetes
entries.protocols: Note: There was a mistranslation between Epithalon and Epithalamin. Epithalamin is an extract of Epithalon and is less concentrated.  The original studies in Russia and Ukraine actually used Epithalamin, that is why the dose is 10 mg.  The equal Epithalon dose is 100 mcg.   The protocols below are adjusted accordingly. 10 mg of Epithalamin = 100 mcg of Epithalon. IM or SQ: • 100 mcg daily for 10 days done twice a year (total of 100 mg) (Dr. Seeds) • 100 mcg every third day until you reach 50 mg total (The Ukrainian Protocol, original study by the Ukraine Academy Medical Sciences)  • 100 mcg every 3 days for 15 days (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog) • 100 mcg three times a week for 3 weeks done once a year (The Ukrainian Protocol, original study by the Ukraine Academy Medical Sciences)   Epithalon is often stacked with Thymalin: IM (some do SQ): • 5 mg thymalin and 5 mg epithalamine daily for 20 days, repeating every 6 months (Jay Campbell)
entries.notes: • There exists a mistranslation between Epithalon and Epithalamin • Epithalamin is an extract of Epitalon and is less concentrated, so need to take 100 mcg of Epithalon instead of 10 mg of Epithalamin • Similar translation issue exists for Thymogen and Thymalin • Long-term results, but they may start showing up after the course is already done
entries.sideEffects: • Diarrhea • Difficulty sleeping • Fatigue • Flu-like symptoms • Headache • Irregular heartbeat • Joint pain • Nausea

entries.name: Frag 176-191
entries.benefits: • Induces fat breakdown  • Prevents new fat growth • Does not affect glucose levels • Helps to strengthen bones • Can improve sleep patterns • Lowering blood sugar levels
entries.protocols: SQ:  • 500 mcg first thing every morning, then wait an hour before food (World of Peptides)
entries.notes: LINK Real Frag is always cloudy • HGH Fragment 176-191 peptide is a modified form of amino acids 176-191 of the human Growth  • Hormone (GH) Polypeptide • Modified version of Frag 176-191 is AOD-9604
entries.sideEffects: • ISR • Sleepiness or lethargic feeling

entries.name: GHK-Cu
entries.benefits: • Enhances skin elasticity • Reduces fine lines and wrinkles • Reverses thinning of aging skin • Prevents aging through its antioxidant effects  • Accelerates wound healing  • Improves lung health  • Fights hair loss  • Accelerates nerve regeneration  • Fights anxiety  • Combats cognitive dysfunction  • Fights Inflammation
entries.protocols: SQ:  • 1-2 mg daily for 6 weeks, can be split in half for morning/day. Follow by at least 4 weeks off. Can repeat the cycle 3-4 times a year (Dr.Seeds)  Topical:  • 1-2 pumps daily for 30 days (5% concentration) (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog)
entries.sideEffects: • Copper allergy (careful!) • If anything more than mild local ISR - discontinue the use of the peptide • Dizziness • Fatigue • Headache • Increased appetite • Increased blood pressure • Nausea • Sun sensitivity • The lunula of the nail can turn blue (will revert to normal in a few weeks)

entries.name: Humanin
entries.benefits: • Protects mitochondria from oxidative stress • Used in treatment of bone osteoporosis, cardiovascular diseases, diabetes mellitus, and neurodegenerative diseases • May able to protect against the development of kidney disease and organ (including eyes) inflammation • Can improve longevity
entries.protocols: Treatment length: 30 days SQ: • 1 mg daily  Note: The protocol is not clearly established yet. This one is frequently mentioned on Peppy’s, Discord servers.
entries.notes: • Also known as Humanin - HN • There is a modified/concentrated version Humanin G (HN-G). The protocol is in mcg, but not clearly established • Can stack with MOTS-C after SS-31

entries.name: Ibutamoren (MK-677)
entries.benefits: • Increase lean muscle mass • Increased metabolism • Decreased body fat • Improved energy • Cognitive performance • Increased endurance • Increased IGF-1 • Supports joint health
entries.protocols: Treatment length: 4-6 weeks with at least 4-6 weeks off in-between Oral: • Males: 25 mg  daily before bedtime on an empty stomach  • Females: 12.5 mg daily before bedtime on an empty stomach  (Peptide Therapy, Tailor Made Compounding Catalog, Metabolic Optimization Guide)
entries.notes: • Can make you extremely hungry, so better to eat protein
entries.sideEffects: • Increased appetite • Headaches • Increased blood sugar levels • Increased insulin levels • Increased levels of the stress hormone cortisol • Sleepiness • Mild lower extremity edema • Muscle pain

sources.note: HUMAN (pharmacodynamic duration, not plasma PK). Int J Mol Sci 2026;27:3890 peer-reviewed review: MK-677 'stimulat[es] sustained GH and IGF-1 secretion for ~24 h with a single daily dose', citing Chapman I et al., J Clin Endocrinol Metab 1997;82:3455-63 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/9329386/). Consistent with once-daily oral dosing.

entries.name: Ipamorelin
entries.benefits: • Increased cellular repair and regeneration • collagen production • Improve lean muscle mass • Decrease body fat • Improve sleep • Improve bone density • Anti-aging and improve overall well-being
entries.protocols: Treatment length: no longer than 8 weeks, with equal time off SQ: • 200 mcg nightly (5 nights on, 2 nights off) (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog) • 200-300 mcg one to three times a day (Jay Campbell)  Start at a lower dose due to possible allergic reaction. Inject SLOWLY  Ipamorelin is often stacked with CJC-1295 no DAC: SQ: • 200mcg/200mcg nightly (5 nights on, 2 nights off) (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog) Wait 2-3 hours after eating, and don’t eat for another 30 minutes after injection
entries.notes: • Human growth hormone releasing peptide (GHRP) • GHRP should be paired with CHRH (e.g., Tesamorelin or CJC-1295), as it increases the GHRP effect and you can use a lower dose
entries.sideEffects: • Facial flushing • Feeling warm • Tachycardia (rare, usually short-lived 5 minutes) • ISR • Headaches • Muscle pain • Water retention

sources.note: HUMAN. Gobburu JV, Agerso H, Jusko WJ, Ynddal L. Pharm Res 1999;16(9):1412-6, PK/PD study in healthy male volunteers (15-min IV infusions, 5 dose levels): 'The PK parameters showed dose-proportionality, with a short terminal half-life of 2 hours, a clearance of 0.078 L/h/kg and a volume of distribution at steady-state of 0.22 L/kg.' Confirms the value already in the dataset. Corroborated by Front Endocrinol 2026;17:1822475 (t1/2 ~2 h, human).
Terminal half-life 2 h; GH response is a single episode peaking at 0.67 h and declining exponentially to negligible levels.

entries.name: Retatrutide
entries.benefits: • Triple agonist: GIP + GLP-1 + glucagon receptors • The largest weight reductions reported for an incretin agent in phase 2 trials (up to -24.2% at 48 weeks at 12 mg) • Improved glycemic control in type 2 diabetes (phase 1b/2) • Reduced liver fat in metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (phase 2a) • Increased satiety and reduced food intake • The glucagon arm additionally raises energy expenditure
entries.protocols: INVESTIGATIONAL — retatrutide (LY3437943) is NOT approved by the FDA or EMA. There is no prescribing information and no approved dosing schedule. The schedules below are the doses studied in the published trials, reported for reference only. Phase 2 obesity trial (Jastreboff et al., N Engl J Med 2023;389:514-526): subcutaneous retatrutide once weekly for 48 weeks in adults with obesity, at 1 mg, 4 mg (starting at 2 mg or 4 mg), 8 mg (starting at 2 mg or 4 mg), or 12 mg (starting at 2 mg), versus placebo. Doses were escalated stepwise; the trial reported that gastrointestinal side effects were dose-related and were partially mitigated by the lower 2 mg starting dose rather than 4 mg. Phase 1b in type 2 diabetes (Urva et al., Lancet 2022;400:1869-1881): once-weekly subcutaneous dosing over 12 weeks. Phase 3 (TRIUMPH programme) is ongoing.
entries.notGoodWith: Semaglutide, Semaglutide Oral, Tirzepatide, other GLP-1 receptor agonists (do not combine)
entries.notes: • INVESTIGATIONAL COMPOUND. Not FDA- or EMA-approved. There is no manufacturer label, no approved indication, and no long-term safety data. Everything below comes from published phase 1 and phase 2 trials • Triple GIP / GLP-1 / glucagon receptor agonist developed by Eli Lilly • The ~6-day half-life supports once-weekly subcutaneous dosing, which is what all trials used • Absolute subcutaneous bioavailability has NOT been published, so the bioavailability field is deliberately omitted • Phase 2 saw dose-dependent increases in heart rate that peaked at 24 weeks and then declined • In phase 1, weight reduction after a single dose persisted up to day 43 • Material sold outside a clinical trial is not a regulated pharmaceutical product and its identity, purity and dose are not verified
entries.sideEffects: • Nausea • Vomiting • Diarrhea • Constipation • Gastrointestinal effects were dose-related, mostly mild to moderate, and partially mitigated by a lower starting dose • Dose-dependent increase in heart rate (peaked at 24 weeks, then declined) • Decreased appetite • Long-term safety profile is unknown — the compound is still in phase 3 trials

sources.note: HUMAN. Urva S et al., 'LY3437943, a novel triple GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptor agonist in people with type 2 diabetes: a phase 1b, multicentre, double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomised, multiple-ascending dose trial', Lancet 2022;400(10366):1869-1881, verbatim: 'Median time to tmax was 12-48 h postdose, and the half-life was approximately 6 days.' 6 days = 144 h. Corroborated by Coskun T et al., Cell Metab 2022;34(9):1234-1247 (phase 1 SAD in healthy participants): 'the mean terminal half-life was ~5 to 7 days, thus supporting a once-weekly dosing regimen'.

HUMAN, SUBCUTANEOUS (matches form factor). RANGES ONLY ARE PUBLISHED, and the two trials differ: Urva 2022 (Lancet, type 2 diabetes, multiple ascending dose) reports 'Median time to tmax was 12-48 h postdose'; Coskun 2022 (Cell Metab, healthy participants, single ascending dose) reports Cmax 'occurred between ~1 and 3 days postdose' (24-72 h). 24 h is the value common to both published ranges. Treat as approximate.
~6-day half-life with slow absorption and once-weekly subcutaneous dosing throughout the phase 1/2/3 programme.

Derived from the TRIAL DOSING INTERVAL, not from a duration-of-action study: all published trials (Urva 2022 phase 1b; Jastreboff et al., N Engl J Med 2023;389:514-526 phase 2) dose retatrutide subcutaneously ONCE WEEKLY, and the ~6-day half-life is explicitly described as 'supporting a once-weekly dosing regimen'. For context, the phase 1 SAD study reported that weight reduction after a SINGLE dose persisted up to day 43.
DELIBERATELY OMITTED. No absolute subcutaneous bioavailability has been published for retatrutide in any phase 1/2 report located. Do not populate this field.

entries.name: Selank
entries.benefits: • Anti-anxiety • Improves cognitive function • Boosts immune function • Treats alcohol withdrawal symptoms • Improves liver health
entries.protocols: Intranasal: • 750-1000 mcg daily in the morning(Dr. Seeds)  SQ:  • 100-300 mcg daily in the morning (Dr. Seeds)
entries.notes: • Can be used as an antidepressant, anxiolytic, generalized anxiety disorder (GAD) treatment
entries.sideEffects: • Dizziness • Fatigue • Hair loss • Headaches • Nasal irritations • Nausea • Sore throat

entries.name: Semaglutide
entries.benefits: • Substantial, sustained weight reduction • Improved glycemic control in type 2 diabetes (HbA1c reduction) • Reduced major adverse cardiovascular events in adults with established cardiovascular disease and overweight/obesity • Slows gastric emptying and increases satiety • Reduces risk of sustained eGFR decline and end-stage kidney disease in type 2 diabetes with CKD • Reduced appetite and food cravings
entries.protocols: Semaglutide is a prescription GLP-1 receptor agonist. All schedules below are the manufacturer's approved titration schedules, quoted for reference only. Once-weekly SQ, same day each week, any time of day, with or without meals. Type 2 diabetes (OZEMPIC, FDA prescribing information): • 0.25 mg once weekly for 4 weeks (initiation dose, not for glycemic control) • increase to 0.5 mg once weekly • after at least 4 weeks, may increase to 1 mg once weekly • after at least 4 weeks, may increase to a maximum of 2 mg once weekly. Weight management (WEGOVY, FDA prescribing information): • Weeks 1-4: 0.25 mg once weekly • Weeks 5-8: 0.5 mg • Weeks 9-12: 1 mg • Weeks 13-16: 1.7 mg • Week 17 onward: 2.4 mg (recommended maintenance); a 7.2 mg maximum maintenance dose is available for patients who tolerate 2.4 mg for at least 4 weeks and need additional weight reduction. The label notes that if a dose is not tolerated, escalation may be delayed by 4 weeks.
entries.notGoodWith: Tirzepatide, Retatrutide, Semaglutide Oral, other GLP-1 receptor agonists (do not combine — labels advise against coadministration of two GLP-1 receptor agonists)
entries.notes: • GLP-1 receptor agonist; brand names OZEMPIC (type 2 diabetes) and WEGOVY (weight management) • The long half-life comes from albumin binding, which lowers renal clearance and protects against DPP-4 degradation • Semaglutide stays in the circulation for about 5 weeks after the last dose, so effects and side effects clear slowly • Steady state is reached after 4-5 weeks of weekly dosing • Boxed warning: risk of thyroid C-cell tumors (contraindicated with personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN 2) • If a dose is missed it can be taken within 5 days; after that, skip it • Delays gastric emptying, which can affect absorption of other oral medications • Prescription-only; this entry describes approved product labeling, not a recommendation
entries.sideEffects: • Nausea • Vomiting • Diarrhea • Abdominal pain • Constipation • Risk of thyroid C-cell tumors (boxed warning) • Pancreatitis • Gallbladder disease • Hypoglycemia when combined with insulin or a sulfonylurea • Acute kidney injury (usually via dehydration from GI losses) • Diabetic retinopathy complications • Increased heart rate • Pulmonary aspiration during general anesthesia or deep sedation • Injection site reactions

sources.note: FDA label, OZEMPIC (semaglutide) injection, section 12.3: 'With an elimination half-life of approximately 1 week, semaglutide will be present in the circulation for about 5 weeks after the last dose.' 1 week = 168 h.

FDA label, OZEMPIC section 12.3, verbatim: 'Maximum concentration of semaglutide is reached 1 to 3 days post dose.' Identical wording in the WEGOVY label. 48 h is the midpoint of the labeled 24-72 h range; the label publishes a RANGE, not a point estimate.

FDA label, SUBCUTANEOUS route (matches form factor). OZEMPIC section 12.3: 'Absolute bioavailability of semaglutide is 89%.' WEGOVY label: 'Absolute bioavailability of semaglutide is 89% following subcutaneous administration.'
Once-weekly subcutaneous dosing, ~1 week half-life, slow absorption (tmax 1-3 days) - matches the 'depot' definition (weekly+ dosing, sustained exposure).

Derived from the APPROVED DOSING INTERVAL, not from a single-dose duration-of-action study: the FDA label prescribes once-weekly administration and a single dose maintains effect across the 7-day interval. The label also permits taking a missed dose up to 5 days late, which supports a >=7 day window of meaningful action.

entries.name: Semaglutide Oral
entries.benefits: • Improved glycemic control in type 2 diabetes (HbA1c reduction) • Weight reduction • Reduced appetite and increased satiety • Oral route — no injection required • Same GLP-1 receptor agonist molecule as injectable semaglutide
entries.protocols: Prescription oral GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide co-formulated with the absorption enhancer SNAC). All schedules below are the manufacturer's approved titration schedules, quoted for reference only. Administration (FDA prescribing information): take one tablet once daily on an empty stomach in the morning with no more than 4 ounces of water; swallow whole (do not split, crush or chew); wait at least 30 minutes before eating, drinking anything else, or taking other oral medications. Titration (RYBELSUS, FDA prescribing information): • Days 1-30: 3 mg once daily (initiation dose, not effective for glycemic control) • Days 31-60: 7 mg once daily • Day 61 onward: stay at 7 mg, or increase to 14 mg once daily if additional glycemic control is needed. A newer OZEMPIC tablet formulation uses 1.5 mg / 4 mg / 9 mg steps on the same 30-day schedule; the two formulations are NOT interchangeable milligram-for-milligram. WEGOVY tablets (weight management) titrate from 1.5 mg once daily every 30 days to a 25 mg once-daily maintenance dose.
entries.notGoodWith: Semaglutide (injectable), Tirzepatide, Retatrutide, other GLP-1 receptor agonists (do not combine)
entries.halfLife: Approximately 1 week (~168 hours), same as injectable semaglutide
entries.notes: • Brand names RYBELSUS (type 2 diabetes), and WEGOVY tablets / OZEMPIC tablets • Absorption happens predominantly in the STOMACH and is highly sensitive to food, water volume and post-dose fasting time — this is why the empty-stomach, 30-minute-wait rule matters so much • Oral bioavailability is very low (roughly 0.4-1%), which is why the oral milligram doses look so different from the injectable ones • Despite once-daily dosing, the elimination half-life is still about a week, so the drug persists ~5 weeks after the last tablet and steady state takes 4-5 weeks • effectDurationDays is deliberately omitted: no single-dose duration-of-action figure is published for the oral form (it is dosed daily) • Boxed warning: risk of thyroid C-cell tumors • Prescription-only; this entry describes approved product labeling, not a recommendation
entries.sideEffects: • Nausea • Abdominal pain • Diarrhea • Decreased appetite • Vomiting • Constipation • Risk of thyroid C-cell tumors (boxed warning) • Pancreatitis • Gallbladder disease • Hypoglycemia when combined with insulin or a sulfonylurea • Diabetic retinopathy complications • Acute kidney injury • Pulmonary aspiration during general anesthesia or deep sedation

sources.note: FDA label, RYBELSUS / OZEMPIC tablets, section 12.3: 'Semaglutide elimination half-life is approximately one week with an absolute clearance of approximately 0.04 L/hour in patients with type 2 diabetes. Semaglutide is present in the circulation for about five weeks after the last semaglutide tablet dose.' 1 week = 168 h. A published clinical-pharmacology analysis (Clin Pharmacokinet, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8505367/) reports the mean terminal half-life more precisely as 153-161 h.
FDA label, ORAL route (matches form factor). RYBELSUS section 12.3, verbatim: 'Semaglutide maximum concentration was reached approximately 1-hour after oral administration of semaglutide tablets.'

FDA label, ORAL route (matches form factor). RYBELSUS section 12.3, verbatim: 'Semaglutide estimated absolute bioavailability was approximately: 0.4% to 1% after oral administration of 3 mg, 7 mg and 14 mg of RYBELSUS; 1% to 2% after oral administration of 1.5 mg, 4 mg and 9 mg of OZEMPIC tablets.' 0.007 (0.7%) is the midpoint of the labeled 0.4-1% RYBELSUS range; the label publishes a RANGE, and the newer OZEMPIC-tablet formulation is higher (1-2%).

Judgement call flagged for the maintainer: oral semaglutide is absorbed FAST (tmax ~1 h) and dosed DAILY, so it is not a depot in the release-limited sense, but it is eliminated slowly (~1 week). 'longActing' is the closest of the four allowed values; 'depot' would wrongly imply slow absorption.

entries.name: Semax
entries.benefits: • Improves heart health • Boosts immunity  • Accelerates wound healing  • Improves brain health and performance (memory, attention and learning capacity) • Lowers the risk of stroke  • Improves mood
entries.protocols: Intranasal: • 750-1000 mcg daily in the morning(Dr. Seeds)  SQ:  • 100-300 mcg daily in the morning (Dr. Seeds)
entries.notes: • Nootropic peptide
entries.sideEffects: • Higher doses can lead to desensitization • Hair loss • Discoloration of the nasal cavity • Increase in blood sugar levels of diabetic patients

entries.name: Sermorelin
entries.benefits: • Reduced body fat • Increased energy and vitality • Improved mental sharpness • Increased strength and lean muscle mass
entries.protocols: SQ:  • 300-500 mcg nightly for 30 days (Peptide Therapy), or up to 6 months (World of Peptides)
entries.notes: • Also known as GRF 1-29 (“Old” CJC-1295)

sources.note: HUMAN. Soule S, King JA, Millar RP. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 1994;79(4):1208-11: in 10 normal men given constant IV infusion, 'The disappearance half-time of the D-Ala2 analog was 6.7 +/- 0.5, whereas that of GHRH-(1-29)-NH2 was 4.3 +/- 1.4 min'. Sermorelin IS GHRH-(1-29)-NH2, so 4.3 min = 0.072 h. Corroborated by Front Endocrinol 2026;17:1822475 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42395176/), which lists sermorelin 't1/2 (as disappearance half-time) ~4.3 +/- 1.4 min' as human data. The dataset's current 6-minute value could not be sourced.
Half-life of minutes.

entries.name: Snap-8
entries.benefits: • Reduce deep wrinkles and fine lines • Improve skin elasticity
entries.protocols: Topical: • Reconstitute 10 mg Snap-8 with 1 ml BAC water • Let it sit for 20+ minutes • Add to the 30 ml of rich serum or cream (see Notes column for examples) • Apply to the area of the skin where wrinkles are present, such as the forehead, around the eyes, and the mouth • Usually applied once or twice daily. Specific instructions vary depending on the product used
entries.goodWith: HA, Matrixyl, GHK-CU, Ceramides, Vitamin E, Vitamin B, Argireline
entries.notGoodWith: High PH, AHA, Vitamin C, Retinol, Vitamin A
entries.notes: Can add to:  • Ordinary Buffet • Ordinary Agirilene • Olay Regenerist Night Recovery Cream • ELF Holy Hydration • CeraVe Skin Renewing Night Cream • Cetaphil Deep Hydration • Neutrogena Hydroboost

entries.name: SS-31
entries.benefits: • Repair cells • Macular degeneration • Muscle weakness • Energy • Cognitive function • Improves cardiovascular health • Prevents kidney injury • Treats lung injury
entries.protocols: SQ:  • 4 mg daily for 10-20 days • 2 mg on the first day, then 4 mg daily for 12 days (for 50 mg total) (Reddit)
entries.notes: • AKA Elampretide • Good to follow with MOTS-C after SS-31

sources.note: HUMAN, FDA label. FORZINITY (elamipretide, = SS-31), Stealth BioTherapeutics, section 12.3: 'The absolute bioavailability following subcutaneous administration is approximately 92%.' Route matches the entry's Injectable/SC form factor.

HUMAN, FDA label. FORZINITY section 12.3, verbatim: 'Maximum elamipretide concentrations were reached between 0.5 to 1 hour after subcutaneous administration.' 0.75 h is the midpoint of that labeled range; the label gives a range, not a point estimate.

entries.name: TB-500
entries.benefits: • Healing and tissue repair • Muscle atrophy treatment • Cardiovascular disease treatment • Provides pain relief • Possesses strong anti-inflammatory properties • Reducing consequences of aging and viral infection
entries.protocols: SQ:  • 750 mcg daily for 20 days (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog) • 1 mg twice a week (World of Peptides) • 300 mcg to 1 mg daily for no longer than 3 months, followed by 1 month off (Dr. Seeds) • 450 mcg daily for 30 days (Peptide Dosing Guide) • 2.0-2.5 mg every other day (Jay Campbell)
entries.notes: •  Frag 17-23 • TB-500 is the active region of Thymosin Beta-4, but some call TB-500 Thymosin Beta-4 as well

entries.name: Tesamorelin
entries.benefits: • Reduce triglycerides, liver and visceral fat • Decrease middle fat • Improve bone mineral density • Supports muscle metabolism • Benefits cardiovascular health • Can improve cognitive function • Depending on the needs, may be better than CJC-1295
entries.protocols: Treatment length: No longer than 60 days followed by equal time off Wait 2-3 hours after eating, and don’t eat for another 30 minutes after injection SQ:  • 1 mg 5-6 days a week before bed (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog) • 1 mg daily (Dr. Seeds) • 1 mg in the morning (fasted) and 1 mg at bedtime (at least 90 minutes after last meal) (Jay Campbell) • 2 mg daily (Studies) • 200 mcg at bedtime for cognition, 5 days on, 2 days off for 20 weeks  Tesamorelin is often stacked with Ipamorelin: SQ: • 1 mg tesamorelin right before bed (at least 90 mnutes after food) and 200 mcg ipamorelin in the morning (Jay Campbell)
entries.notes: • Human growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH). GHRH should be paired with CHRP, as it increases the GHRP effect and you can use a lower dose • Brand name - EGRIFTA • Developed to treat HIV lipodystrophy
entries.sideEffects: • ISR • Difficulty breathing • Dizziness • Elevations in blood sugar levels • Fast heartbeat • Hives • Joint pain • Night sweats • Numbness • Rash • Vomiting

sources.note: HUMAN, FDA label. EGRIFTA SV section 12.3: 'Mean elimination half-life (t1/2) of tesamorelin was 8 minutes in healthy subjects after single dose subcutaneous administration of the 1.4 mg of EGRIFTA SV.' 8 min = 0.13 h. CONFLICT: the older EGRIFTA formulation and the 2026 review Front Endocrinol 2026;17:1822475 report t1/2 ~26-38 min, which is what the dataset's current 30-minute value reflects. Both are label-derived; 8 min is the current label.
HUMAN, FDA label. EGRIFTA SV / EGRIFTA WR section 12.3: 'The median peak plasma tesamorelin concentration (Tmax) was 0.15 h in both populations' (healthy subjects and HIV-infected patients).

HUMAN, FDA label, SUBCUTANEOUS route (matches form factor). EGRIFTA SV section 12.3 verbatim: 'The absolute bioavailability of tesamorelin after subcutaneous administration of a 2 mg dose of EGRIFTA was determined to be less than 4% in healthy adult subjects.' 0.04 is the label's UPPER BOUND ('less than 4%'), not a point estimate.
Half-life of minutes on any of the published estimates (8 min or 26-38 min).

entries.name: Tesofensine
entries.benefits: • Appetite suppressant • Decreases triglycerides • Increased basal metabolic rate (BMR • Sustained weight loss (i.e., fat tissue loss, waist reduction) • Improves cognitive health  • Improves mood • Increases energy levels
entries.protocols: Oral:  500 mcg daily in the early morning (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog, Jay Campbell) (some start at a smaller dose)
entries.notes: • Some use with Tirzepatide/Semaglutide/Retatrutide or during the breaks, while on maintenance • Doesn’t seem to have any withdrawal effects
entries.sideEffects: • Constipation • Diarrhea • Dry mouth • Headache • Changes in blood pressure (increase blood pressure) • Increased heart rate • Insomnia • Nausea

sources.note: HUMAN. Population PK modelling of NS2330 (tesofensine) and its metabolite, Br J Clin Pharmacol: 'long half-lives of 234 h (NS2330) and 374 h (M1)'; the paper also describes 'markedly long apparent half-lives in humans of ~200 h'. 234 h (~9.75 days) is the modelled parent-compound value. Note the active metabolite M1 is even longer (374 h), so a single-compartment curve will understate the tail.
HUMAN, ORAL route (matches form factor). Verbatim: 'The maximum plasma concentration was reached after 6-8 h.' 7 h is the midpoint of the published range.
HUMAN, ORAL route (matches form factor). Verbatim: 'The absolute bioavailability of NS2330 was estimated to be >90%' (comparison of separate IV and oral studies). 0.9 is the published LOWER BOUND.

Judgement call flagged for the maintainer: tesofensine is dosed orally once daily and is rapidly absorbed (tmax 6-8 h), so it is not a depot, but its ~9.75-day half-life sits just above the stated 1-7 day 'longActing' band and it accumulates for weeks before steady state. 'longActing' is the closest of the four allowed values.

entries.name: Thymalin/Thimogen
entries.benefits: • Treatment of immune disorders • An anti-aging agent • Improves respiratory and cardiovascular health • Enhanced anti-inflammatory properties • Potential anti-tumor activity
entries.protocols: Treatment length: no longer if 10 days if taken daily, no longer than 10 weeks if taken weekly. Preferably no more than 100 mg in a cycle. Can do two cycles a year. Note: Thymalin is an extract of Thymogen. 10 mg of Thymalin = 100 mcg of Thymogen. 30 min before Thymalin take 30 mg of zinc  IM (some do SQ): • For treating immune disorders: 5-20 mg daily for 3-10 days (manufacturer’s instructions) • For anti-aging effects: 2-5 mg daily (World of Peptides) • For anti-aging effects: 5-10 mg daily (manufacturer’s instructions, Jay Campbell) • For anti-aging effects:10 mg once or twice a week (Reddit)  Thymalin is often stacked with Epithalon: IM (some do SQ): • 5 mg thymalin and 5 mg epithalamin daily for 20 days, repeating every 6 months (Jay Campbell)
entries.notes: • Thymalin is an extract of Thymogen and is more concentrated, so need to take 100 mcg of Thymogen instead of 10 mg Thymalin • Similar to Epithalamin vs Epitalon

entries.name: Thymosin Alpha-1
entries.benefits: • Eradicates bacteria, viruses, and fungi • Boosts immune system • Suppresses cancer and tumor growth • Accelerates the wound healing process  • Fights inflammation  • Increases vaccine effectiveness
entries.protocols: SQ:  • 450 mcg daily for 30 days (Peptide Dosing Guide) • 90 mcg daily for 1 month (for a total of 5 mg) (Tailor Made Compounding Catalog) • 1.5 mg every third day for two weeks for viral infection or up to three months for HIV, cancer, Hepatitis B/C, or complicated immune suppression (Dr. Seeds)
entries.notes: • FDA approved • Some physicians are using thymosin for chronic fatigue, Lyme disease, autoimmune function
entries.sideEffects: • Fatigue • Fever • Low white blood cells • Muscle aches • Nausea • Vomiting

sources.note: HUMAN. Ancell CD, Phipps J, Young L. 'Thymosin alpha-1.' Am J Health Syst Pharm 2001;58(10):879-85 (peer-reviewed drug monograph): 'TA1 is rapidly absorbed, achieving peak serum concentrations within two hours. Blood levels return to baseline within 24 hours, and the serum half-life is approximately 2 hours.' Confirms the value already in the dataset.

HUMAN, subcutaneous (the route used for thymalfasin/ZADAXIN, matching the entry's Injectable form factor): 'achieving peak serum concentrations within two hours'. This is an upper bound ('within two hours'), not a measured median.
Serum half-life ~2 h; blood levels back to baseline within 24 h.

entries.name: Thymosin Beta-4
entries.benefits: • Boosts the immune system • Wound healing • Tissue repair • Reduce inflammation • Enhance collagen deposition • Improves heart health • Improves liver health  • Improves eye health  • Promotes nerve regeneration  • Improves brain health  • Increases flexibility
entries.notes: • TB-500 is the active region of Thymosin Beta-4 • Currently being trialed as a potential therapy for HIV, AIDS, and Influenza • Often prescribed for acute injury, surgical repair and for senior athletes
entries.sideEffects: • Dizziness • Headache • Lethargy • Nausea • Tiredness

sources.note: HUMAN but INTRAVENOUS. First-in-human, randomized, double-blind, single- and multiple-dose phase I study of recombinant human thymosin beta-4 (NL005) in healthy Chinese volunteers: 'Among the cohorts, the Tmax was from 3 to 15 minutes, and t1/2 was from 0.5 to 2.08 hours' across 0.25-25 microg/kg IV. Half-life is DOSE-DEPENDENT: 0.5 h at the lowest dose, 2.084 h at the highest. 2.08 h is the top-of-range (highest dose) value; the maintainer may prefer to store the range. Corroborated by Ruff D et al., Ann N Y Acad Sci 2010;1194:223-9 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20536472/), an IV single/multiple-dose study in healthy volunteers reporting dose-proportional PK and 'an increasing half-life with increasing dose' (no numeric t1/2 in the abstract).

entries.name: Tirzepatide
entries.benefits: • Large, sustained weight reduction • Improved glycemic control in type 2 diabetes • Dual GIP + GLP-1 receptor agonism (adds the GIP arm on top of GLP-1) • Improves obstructive sleep apnea in adults with obesity • Enhances glucose-dependent insulin secretion and reduces glucagon • Increased satiety and reduced food intake
entries.protocols: Prescription dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist. All schedules below are the manufacturer's approved titration schedules, quoted for reference only. Once-weekly SQ, any time of day, with or without meals; rotate injection sites. Type 2 diabetes (MOUNJARO, FDA prescribing information): • Start at 2.5 mg once weekly for 4 weeks (initiation dose, not intended for glycemic control) • Increase to 5 mg once weekly • If additional glycemic control is needed, increase in 2.5 mg increments after at least 4 weeks on the current dose • Maximum 15 mg once weekly. Weight management / OSA (ZEPBOUND, FDA prescribing information): • Same 2.5 mg starting dose for 4 weeks, then 5 mg, then 2.5 mg increments after at least 4 weeks • Maintenance 5 mg, 10 mg or 15 mg once weekly (10 mg or 15 mg for obstructive sleep apnea) • Maximum 15 mg once weekly. The labels note that if a maintenance dose is not tolerated, a lower maintenance dose can be considered.
entries.notGoodWith: Semaglutide, Semaglutide Oral, Retatrutide, other GLP-1 receptor agonists (do not combine)
entries.notes: • Dual GIP and GLP-1 receptor agonist; brand names MOUNJARO (type 2 diabetes) and ZEPBOUND (weight management, obstructive sleep apnea) • Contains a C20 fatty diacid that binds albumin and prolongs the half-life, which is what enables once-weekly dosing • Steady state is reached after about 4 weeks of once-weekly dosing • Boxed warning: risk of thyroid C-cell tumors (contraindicated with personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN 2) • The gastric-emptying effect is strongest after the first 5 mg dose and diminishes with subsequent doses • A missed dose can be taken within 4 days (96 hours); after that, skip it • May reduce the effectiveness of oral contraceptives • Prescription-only; this entry describes approved product labeling, not a recommendation
entries.sideEffects: • Nausea • Diarrhea • Vomiting • Constipation • Abdominal pain • Dyspepsia • Decreased appetite • Injection site reactions • Fatigue • Hypersensitivity reactions • Eructation • Hair loss • Gastroesophageal reflux disease • Risk of thyroid C-cell tumors (boxed warning) • Pancreatitis • Gallbladder disease • Hypoglycemia when combined with insulin or a sulfonylurea • Acute kidney injury • Pulmonary aspiration during general anesthesia or deep sedation

sources.note: FDA label, MOUNJARO section 12.3: 'The apparent population mean clearance of tirzepatide is 0.061 L/h with an elimination half-life of approximately 5 days, enabling once-weekly dosing.' 5 days = 120 h. CONFLICT (minor): the ZEPBOUND label (https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=487cd7e7-434c-4925-99fa-aa80b1cc776b) states 'The elimination half-life is approximately 5-6 days in patients with overweight or obesity, and in patients with OSA and obesity' - i.e. 120-144 h in the weight-management population.

FDA label, ZEPBOUND section 12.3, verbatim: 'Following subcutaneous administration, the median time (range) to maximum plasma concentration of tirzepatide is 24 hours (8 to 72 hours).' 24 h is the published MEDIAN (the MOUNJARO label gives only the 8-72 h range).
FDA label, SUBCUTANEOUS route (matches form factor). MOUNJARO and ZEPBOUND section 12.3: 'The mean absolute bioavailability of tirzepatide following subcutaneous administration is 80%.'
Once-weekly subcutaneous dosing, ~5 day half-life, slow absorption (median tmax 24 h) - matches the 'depot' definition.

Derived from the APPROVED DOSING INTERVAL, not from a single-dose duration-of-action study: the FDA label prescribes once-weekly administration and a single dose maintains effect across the 7-day interval. The label also permits taking a missed dose up to 4 days late.
